Title: Live point-by-point scoring
Description: Tap to award points to either team in real time. The interface is optimised for one-handed use during active play.

Title: Set management
Description: Automatic set tracking with configurable winning conditions. TournaQ handles the transition between sets without manual input.

Title: Score correction
Description: Undo the last point at any time. Mistakes during fast play can be corrected immediately without losing match state.

Title: Timeout tracking
Description: Record timeouts per team per set, in line with standard beach volleyball rules.

Title: Match result & history
Description: Completed matches are saved locally on the device with full score history per set.

Title: Player point statistics
Description: Track individual player contributions within a match — points won, errors, and serve effectiveness.

Title: Historical match analytics
Description: Aggregated statistics and trends across matches and seasons for teams and individual players.

Title: Tournament creation & setup
Description: Define the event name, date, venue, number of courts, and participant list before play begins.

Title: Match scheduling
Description: Assign matches to courts and time slots. Handle court changes and late adjustments without manual recalculation.

Title: Injury & withdrawal handling
Description: Mark teams as withdrawn mid-tournament and automatically adjust brackets and standings without corrupting results already recorded.

Title: Singleplayer and Team Competitions
Description: Support for scramble draws, mixed-gender formats, and other non-standard competition structures common in recreational beach volleyball and trainings.

Title: Share Scorecards and Results via QR Code
Description: Generate QR Codes to share Scorecards and Results even when offline.

Title: Multi-court parallel tracking
Description: Run multiple simultaneous matches across courts within a single event, with results feeding into shared standings.

Title: Participant scaling (100+ players)
Description: Infrastructure and UI optimisations to support large events with over 100 participants and dozens of simultaneous courts.

Title: Mobile-first design
Description: Optimised for use on smartphones. Touch targets, layout, and navigation are designed for one-handed use during a live match.

Title: Offline-first local storage
Description: All match data is stored on-device using local storage. TournaQ works fully without an internet connection — no signal required on court.

Title: Portrait & landscape orientation
Description: The scoring interface adapts to both portrait and landscape orientations to suit different holding preferences and device types.

Title: Large screen tournament view
Description: A dedicated layout for larger screens that shows multiple courts or full bracket views simultaneously — designed for tournament control rooms.

Title: Cast to display
Description: Project the live scoreboard or bracket view to an external screen or TV at the venue — for spectators and team benches.

Title: Multi-device sync
Description: Keep match data in sync across multiple devices at the same event — so scorekeepers and organisers always see the same state.

Title: Live court score display
Description: A consolidated view showing current scores across all active courts — updated in real time as scorekeepers enter points.

Title: Real-time standings
Description: Group standings and bracket positions update automatically as match results come in, without manual entry or refresh.

Title: Spectator view
Description: A read-only view of live scores, standings, and match schedules accessible to spectators — no account or app required.

Title: Public tournament URL
Description: Each event gets a shareable URL that spectators, players, and coaches can open on any device to follow the tournament live.

Title: Live Scoreboard access for Referees / Players / Tournament Administrators
Description: Manage tournaments paperless with live Scorecard and Results synchronization across all enrolled participants.

Title: Live bracket visualisation
Description: An interactive bracket diagram that updates live as matches complete — shareable with teams and displayable on venue screens.

Title: Public tournament archive
Description: Searchable archive of past events with final results, standings, and statistics accessible to anyone.

Title: Organiser accounts
Description: Dedicated organiser accounts with persistent access to event history, team data, and tournament configurations.

Title: Role-based access control
Description: Assign different permission levels to users — e.g. head organiser, scorekeeper, referee, read-only viewer — so each person sees and can do only what's appropriate for their role.

Title: Multi-organiser events
Description: Allow multiple people to co-manage a single event — useful for large tournaments with separate court managers or a dedicated administration team.

Title: Event-level permissions
Description: Control who can view, edit, or publish results for a specific event — independently of organisation-wide roles.

Title: Audit log
Description: A timestamped log of actions taken during an event — score changes, bracket edits, withdrawals — for dispute resolution and record-keeping.
